chunk_summary: This section outlines tele-nutrition guidelines in India, focusing on growth standards, malnutrition thresholds, and supplementation protocols, referencing WHO and IAP growth charts, and MoHFW operational guidance for SAM management and micronutrient schedules.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of WHO and IAP growth charts in tele-nutrition for Indian children.
Identify malnutrition thresholds and criteria for moderate and severe acute malnutrition (SAM).
Learn the age-specific micronutrient supplementation and prophylaxis schedules in India.
Apply MoHFW operational guidelines for SAM management in tele-nutrition services.
key_facts: WHO child growth standards are used for children aged 0–60 months, with preterm-specific tools for preterm infants.
IAP 2015 growth charts are recommended for Indian children aged 5–18 years.
Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) is defined as weight-for-height between -3 and -2 z-scores and/or MUAC ≥115 mm and <125 mm for children aged 6–59 months.
Severe acute malnutrition (SAM) includes MUAC <115 mm, very low weight-for-height, or bilateral pitting oedema.
Vitamin A prophylaxis is scheduled for 9 doses between 9 months and 5 years, as per NHM guidelines.
National Deworming Day recommends albendazole for ages 1–19 years, with a caution to avoid deworming when sick.
reference_quote: WHO child growth standards support assessment from 0–60 months, and preterm tools should be used for preterm infants until transition to term standards.
